metadata.title: Why giving a model more tools makes it choose worse
metadata.purpose: Every tool description is context the model must weigh; a crowded toolset adds noise that dilutes selection, so filtering to intent-relevant tools restores focus without losing capability.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.still_lanes: c2v (all tools → filtered subset), raster (tool list before/after)
metadata.visual_object: A wide tool tree pruning down to a small intent-matched subset.
metadata.hook: Adding tools should expand what an agent can do; past a point it degrades which tool the agent picks — and the fix is to hide tools, not add them.

beats.narration_text: "Create a GitHub repository." With all ~50 tools exposed the model reaches for `create_pull_request`; with only the `repos` toolset enabled it lands on `create_repository` immediately.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Enabling every tool should predict better coverage; instead accuracy drops — why does tool proliferation degrade tool choice?
Adding tools should expand what an agent can do; past a point it degrades which tool the agent picks — and the fix is to hide tools, not add them.
Every tool description is context the model must weigh; a crowded toolset adds noise that dilutes selection, so filtering to intent-relevant tools restores focus without losing capability.
beats.visual: A wide tool tree pruning down to a small intent-matched subset.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: A 200-tool interface makes ~3× the tool-pick errors of a 6-tool one; intent filtering to the 6 relevant tools recovers accuracy.
Every tool description is context the model must weigh; a crowded toolset adds noise that dilutes selection, so filtering to intent-relevant tools restores focus without losing capability.
